framework.title: Revenue Leak Diagnostic Engine Decision Logic
framework.description: A governed interpretive decision layer that converts scored questionnaire outputs into stable diagnostic states, leak-class resolution, intervention priority, and report-ready explanatory structures.
framework.purpose: To ensure that structural scores are transformed into decision outputs through explicit, reviewable, non-decorative logic rather than ad hoc interpretation.
framework.methodology_note: This layer does not create new evidence. It interprets declared questionnaire outputs using governed thresholds, tie-handling rules, priority resolution, and reporting templates.

invalid_input_policy.reason: v1 does not permit interpretive output from incomplete structural scoring.

primary_resolution.rule: The leak class with the lowest normalized leak signal score is the default primary leak class.
primary_resolution.explanation: If the lowest class score is at least 5 points lower than the next candidate, it is treated as materially primary.

secondary_resolution.rule: The second-lowest normalized leak signal score becomes a secondary leak class only when it remains diagnostically meaningful.
secondary_resolution.explanation: Secondary state is permitted when the second class remains weak enough to matter and is not merely noise.

dual_state_resolution.rule: If the two lowest leak-class scores differ by 3 points or less, the result may enter a dual-state diagnostic mode.
dual_state_resolution.explanation: v1 allows interpretive duality where forcing singularity would create false precision.

rules.name: message_path_alignment_override
rules.override_condition: If LEAK-06 is within 5 points of the lowest leak score and total_integrity_score <= 69, LEAK-06 may take strategic priority.
rules.reason: Misalignment can contaminate downstream interpretation by breaking continuity before local remediation has leverage.

bands.meaning: The dimension appears structurally sound and is unlikely to be the dominant failure source.
The dimension is generally stable but contains meaningful weakness.
The dimension is meaningfully underperforming and may contribute directly to leakage.
The dimension is seriously compromised and likely contributes heavily to leakage.
The dimension is structurally failing and cannot be treated as operationally stable.

dimension_state_logic.weakest_dimension_rule: The lowest-scoring dimension becomes the weakest dimension.
dimension_state_logic.strongest_dimension_rule: The highest-scoring dimension becomes the strongest dimension.

multi_weakness_rule.explanation: If two or more dimensions score 54 or below, the system may surface a multi-weakness condition.

report_state_logic.strategic_priority_rule: The report must always surface the first intervention priority before discussing secondary optimization opportunities.

priority_buckets.title: Immediate Structural Priority
priority_buckets.meaning: These classes can directly destabilize the interpretive or decision foundation of the path.

priority_buckets.title: High Operational Priority
priority_buckets.meaning: These classes materially suppress performance and should be addressed once higher-order distortion is understood.

priority_order_rules.reason: Misalignment distorts the meaning of downstream behavior and weakens relevance from the start.

priority_order_rules.priority_label: Restore confidence before pushing harder for conversion
Clarify decision logic before increasing persuasive intensity
priority_order_rules.reason: Users cannot act confidently when interpretation remains weak.

priority_order_rules.priority_label: Repair continuity before optimizing local elements
Reduce burden after continuity and decision support are understood
priority_order_rules.reason: Friction overload suppresses movement through process cost.

priority_order_rules.priority_label: Build recovery after the core path is structurally legible
priority_order_rules.reason: Recovery matters most when the primary path has recoverable value.

secondary_output_conditions.reason: Secondary class is not weak enough to justify explicit reporting.

secondary_redundancy_rules.reason: Do not duplicate intervention outputs when both states map to the same plug class.

dual_state_output_rules.reason: Avoid false singularity when two structural failure modes are effectively co-dominant.

plug_recommendation_logic.primary_mapping_policy: The primary leak class maps to the primary plug class using governed leak-to-plug rules from scoring_rules.json.
plug_recommendation_logic.secondary_mapping_policy: A secondary plug class may be surfaced when the secondary leak class remains meaningful and non-redundant.
plug_recommendation_logic.intervention_order_policy: Primary plug class must always be shown before any secondary recommendation.

override_ordering_rules.note: Alignment repair takes interpretive precedence over local conversion tuning.
Trust repair takes precedence when confidence is structurally insufficient.

executive_openers.sound_state: The path appears structurally stable across its primary integrity dimensions.
executive_openers.minor_leakage_state: The path remains viable, but meaningful structural leakage is present.
executive_openers.moderate_weakness_state: The path contains material structural weakness that is likely suppressing conversion integrity.
executive_openers.serious_leakage_state: The path is materially compromised by major structural failure patterns.
executive_openers.critical_failure_state: The path is structurally unstable enough that reliable conversion integrity cannot be assumed.

weakest_dimension_templates.trust_integrity: The weakest structural dimension is trust integrity, indicating that confidence support is lagging behind commitment demand.
weakest_dimension_templates.flow_continuity: The weakest structural dimension is flow continuity, indicating that movement is not being carried cleanly through the path.
weakest_dimension_templates.decision_clarity: The weakest structural dimension is decision clarity, indicating that users are under-supported at the moment of interpretation and choice.
weakest_dimension_templates.friction_containment: The weakest structural dimension is friction containment, indicating that process burden is eroding movement or completion.
weakest_dimension_templates.recovery_readiness: The weakest structural dimension is recovery readiness, indicating that interrupted intent is not being reclaimed effectively.

primary_secondary_templates.template: The path appears to be losing commitment through a combination of weak confidence architecture and under-supported decision clarity.
The path appears to be losing momentum through continuity failure reinforced by unnecessary burden.
The path appears to be under-explaining the decision while also failing to support confidence strongly enough.
The system appears weak not only in reclaiming lost intent, but also in sustaining confidence when users hesitate.
The path appears to break continuity between entry promise and decision logic, creating ambiguity downstream.
The path appears to violate expectation continuity early and then lose movement stability through the journey.

interpretation_templates.fallback_secondary_template: A secondary structural weakness is also present and should be considered after the primary failure mode is addressed.
interpretation_templates.single_state_template: The dominant structural weakness appears sufficiently distinct to justify a singular primary diagnosis.

methodology_boundary_messages.message: This result is a structural diagnostic, not a financial forecast.
This result is derived from declared funnel conditions rather than observed behavioral telemetry.
The output is intended to guide intervention priority, not guarantee commercial uplift.
